title: About
text:
This is small space I’ve built for myself to share what I’ve discovered in the field of Data Science and coding in general. I have always been curious about the way things work; I love discovering new fields and studying the governing principles within them. My undergraduate background is in Interdisciplinary Medical Sciences, and my career started in clinical research of haematologic malaginancies. However, over time, I realized that my true passion lies in the realm of data.
Through independent study and the generous guidance of mentors, I taught myself the fundamentals of coding and analytics. I began building my own projects with a focus on practicing Data Engineering and Machine Learning Engineering concepts. My work includes developing deep learning solutions, such as a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) using Keras and TensorFlow, to detect diabetic retinopathy. My portfolio also features full-stack applications, such as interactive dashboards for airline data and NYC traffic collisions using Streamlit and Python.
Currently, I am privileged to be pursuing my Master of Data Science at the University of British Columbia. I am actively seeking opportunities in Data Science, Machine Learning Engineering, and Data Analysis where I can bridge the gap between raw information and scalable, actionable intelligence.

title: About
text:
Chess
I first picked up chess during my undergraduate program as a competitive and mentally stimulating outlet. While my rating is quite low, chess has been a pleasant amateur hobby of mine over the last few years. At my level, the schema of memorized openings and theory is rather limited; therefore, my games quickly diverge from known sequences and into uncharted territory. I really appreciate the abundance of novel challenges in every game, and the convenience of online matchmaking has made chess a consistent pastime. I enjoy playing casual games, solving puzzles, and occasionally watching chess content!

title: Jeffrey’s Git Page
text:
My GitHub Page
My GitHub page contains various repositories related to my projects and interests. Feel free to explore and contribute!


Detecting diabetic retinopathy with AI
This project preprocesses and analyzes retina scans with a Convolutional Neural Network and Residual blocks using Keras and Tensorflow 2.0 to improve early detection of retinopathy.


Motor Vehicle Collisions of New York City
This is an interactive web app built using Python, Folium, and Streamlit to visualize NYC traffic accidents. Information is pulled from realtime NYC traffic database and visualized below.


Stock Exchange Revenue Dashboard


Website Info
This is a Quarto website.
To learn more about Quarto websites visit https://quarto.org/docs/websites.

title: About
text:
Brazilian Jiu-jitsu
I was first exposed to Brazilian Jiu-jitsu (BJJ) during my undergraduate program when my roommate dove into the sport. As the nearest friend of similar size and build, I was roped into his newfound interest as the defacto sparring partner. Not to say that I didn’t enjoy the experience, I found BJJ to be an very rewarding physical activity with which I’m continuously fascinated.
Possibly owing to it’s Brazilian influence, BJJ had a more casual environment and culture than its Japanese roots. The ease and friendliness of the sport was more accessible to newcomers and made it easier for me to partipate. As a martial art, BJJ had a surprisingly amount of technical and strategic depth to each match. It also demanded high amounts of energy and exertion for for each sparring session, otherwise known as a roll. This made BJJ a very physically and mentally engaging exercise that I could enjoy in very discrete sections of time.
